channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Rethinking Transitional Justice in Bosnia | Foreign Policy
item.description: Western assessments miss how economic ties have quietly advanced reconciliation.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian drone strike on empty Baltic fuel depot prompts top-level resignation – in Latvia | Defense News
item.description: A Ukrainian official said Russia is spoofing Ukraine's drones, diverting them to strike nearby Baltic nations instead of targets in Russia.

item.title: 📍 Governments Can’t Agree on What AI Actually Is | Foreign Policy
item.description: Without clear definitions, governance is impossible.

item.title: 📍 Latvian Defense Minister resigns, following lagging response to drone incursions | Breaking Defense
item.description: Military drone detection systems failed to identify an incoming aircraft, and mobile warnings were not sent to residents’ phones until approximately an hour after it had already crashed.

item.title: 📍 There’s No Such Thing as Climate Policy | Foreign Policy
item.description: It’s time to rethink top-down attempts at environmental progress.

item.title: 📍 US investors warm to Ukrainian defense startups—but export laws slow cooperation | Defense One
item.description: One firm says month-long wait for approval puts it at a competitive disadvantage.

item.title: 🌍 Ankara’s Crossroads: Rearmament, Risk, and the Prospect of War with Israel | War on the Rocks
item.description: Talk of war in the Middle East abounds on social media, but not just about the one with Iran. A small, but significant, portion of this chatter relates to a conflict that has not begun. For more than a year now, pundits and politicians have warned of a potential clash between…

item.title: 📍 Conflict, CASEVAC, and the Golden Hour in the Age of Persistent Surveillance | War on the Rocks
item.description: Editor’s Note: This article contains frank descriptions of battlefield conditions, including accounts of soldiers dying by suicide following injury. Western battlefield casualty evacuation doctrine is built on assumptions forged during the “Global War on Terror.” Central among…

item.title: 📍 Aung San Suu Kyi’s Move to House Arrest Is a Calculated Deception | The Diplomat
item.description: Myanmar’s military regime wants the world to believe it is changing. It is not.

item.title: 🕵️ Former Thai PM Thaksin Shinawatra Released From Prison on Parole | The Diplomat
item.description: Following his party's worst electoral result in more than two decades, it remains to be seen if the veteran politician can mount another political comeback.

item.title: 📍 Inland Rail: Australia talks about, but fails at, nation building | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Australian government’s decision to curtail the Inland Rail Project was politically brave. Governments rarely walk away from troubled megaprojects once billions have been spent and expectations have hardened. Yet the real issue sits well ...

item.title: 🌍 Where Are The Carriers As Of May 11, 2026: 20 Warships Enforce Iran Blockade | The War Zone
item.description: Meanwhile, USS Nimitz has made a stop in Rio as part of its circumnavigation of South America and repositioning cruise to Norfolk. The post Where Are The Carriers As Of May 11, 2026: 20 Warships Enforce Iran Blockade appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 EDGE CEO talks company’s role in Iran conflict, plus future partnerships | Breaking Defense
item.description: EDGE Managing Director and CEO Hamad Al Marar sat down with Breaking Defense to discuss UAV technology and future endeavors for the company.

item.title: 🌍 The Winners and Losers of the Iran Energy Shock | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How the war created a new geopolitical divide.

item.title: 🌏 The Stakes of Trump vs. Xi | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How the summit could change the course of U.S.-China competition.

item.title: 📍 ASEAN Leaders Call For Middle East Calm, Reopening of Strait of Hormuz | The Diplomat
item.description: The region has also pledged to move forward with a fuel-sharing agreement to offset shortages of oil from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: 📍 A Nobel economist models how AI rots the information environment | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Most Australians already know something is wrong with our information environment. An Australian National University survey of 20,000 average Australians found disinformation consistently ranked among top national security concerns. Participants rated it as more serious ...

item.title: 🏛️ Now is the time for a US ‘grand deal’ with Azerbaijan | Atlantic Council
item.description: The White House should work with Congress to propose ending a ban on arms sales to Baku in exchange for the release of unjustly detained individuals. The post Now is the time for a US ‘grand deal’ with Azerbaijan appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🕵️ Civic patriotism strengthens our democracy | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Strategic competition now extends far beyond military capability and economic strength. Liberal democracies therefore have a strategic interest in cultivating a confident civic patriotism that strengthens social cohesion, reinforces institutional legitimacy and supports national…

item.title: 📍 Drone Swarms Packed Into Unassuming Containers Sought By DARPA | The War Zone
item.description: Remotely triggered containerized drone swarms, potentially placed behind enemy lines, create a hard-to-defend-against threat from pretty much everywhere. The post Drone Swarms Packed Into Unassuming Containers Sought By DARPA appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (May 4-8, 2026) | Just Security
item.description: U.S.-Israel-Iran War • Russia-Ukraine War • U.S. Military • U.S. Boat Strikes • Series: Syria in Transition • Series: When Guardrails Erode • Violent Extremism • AI Governance • Hungary • International Human Rights • Trump Executive Actions The post Digest of Recent Articles on…

item.title: 🌍 F/A-18 Super Hornet Drops Bombs Down Smokestacks Of Iranian Tankers Running Blockade (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The attack comes as the UAE claims it was struck again by Iran and hours after the U.S. and Iran exchanged blows. The post F/A-18 Super Hornet Drops Bombs Down Smokestacks Of Iranian Tankers Running Blockade (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 So What’s the Strategy for China? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Host Matthew Kroenig is joined by Kurt Campbell, chairman and co-founder of The Asia Group, to discuss former President Joe Biden’s strategy toward China, the Trump administration's strategic approach to Beijing, and Donald Trump’s upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi…

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine ramps up ground robot production to spare soldiers, haul ammo — and rescue grandma | Defense News
item.description: The same unmanned ground systems Ukraine uses to haul ammunition and evacuate wounded soldiers are now pulling civilians out of contested ground.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: Concrete is the new black. The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Russia, Ukraine to enter temporary ceasefire with prisoner exchange, Trump says | Defense News
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that Russia and Ukraine would temporarily halt fighting for three days beginning Saturday.

item.title: 📍 Can Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship survive? | Atlantic Council
item.description: One year into his chancellorship, Friedrich Merz heads a government beset by economic stagnation, low public approval, and coalition tensions. The post Can Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship survive? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Southeast Asian Leaders Tackle Iran War Vulnerabilities | Foreign Policy
item.description: Global market shocks spark calls for a regional power grid and emergency fuel stockpile.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 2: The U.S. announces a drawdown in Germany, Taiwan’s president concludes a foreign visit, and Sudan lobs accusations at Ethiopia.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth aims to cut through the bureaucracy with ‘Deal Team Six’ | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon is utilizing a crew of private sector businessmen to renovate how the department negotiates with defense companies.
